title: The Four-Angled House Where Every Corner Contradicts

features.name: northern facade
features.desc-on-touch: you see a cottage with roses; no roses; a warehouse door; all three at once, the render arguing with itself

features.name: southern prospect
features.desc-on-touch: a shopfront rendered as storehouse, rendered as home, rendered as antenna array — each version equally present

features.name: western threshold
features.desc-on-touch: the door opens onto four rooms at once; you must choose which threshold to cross, or refuse to enter

features.name: eastern argument
features.desc-on-touch: two windows disagree about what they see; the render has drawn them both, refusing to commit to either view

features.name: the contradiction-bearing wall
features.desc-on-touch: a wall that cannot decide its own thickness, color, or purpose; you run your hand across it and feel four different surfaces

inscription: every angle sees a different house; the render cannot choose, so it keeps them all

file.name: Perspective_Studies_Architectural_Disagreement.txt
file.note: four sketches of the same building, each claiming to be the true elevation; the margins fill with corrections that contradict each other

exits.label: back toward the chambers where understanding compares itself
down to where buildings forget their blueprints
further into fracture, where more angles await
